sprint: Sprint 13 -- Pi Run Phase: First Real Data
backlogItem: B-037 (Run phase) + B-043 (auto-sync building block) + B-045 (test harness)

testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 12 close (4/4 stories merged to main@ccb47f2). 2145 Windows fast-suite. Pi aarch64 baseline 1583 + 47 pi_only. Sprint 12 also shipped post-merge fixes 7051ebb (chi-srv-01 IP .120 → .10), d93db32+3aaa5bd (lgpio + apt deps), 74efbdb (jinja2 server dep), a5f21d2+7dcf202 (validate_pi_to_server.sh hang fix; superseded by B-045 / US-191).

sprintNotes: GOAL: Pi mounted in the Eclipse, BT-paired with the OBDLink LX (MAC 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB), reading real engine idle data from the ECU, displaying it correctly. Done-milestone for B-037 Run phase.

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprint 10/11/12). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi work. CIO physically does: mount Pi in car, plug OBDLink LX into car OBD-II port, start engine, BT-pair handshake, eyeball the display.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: Sprint 12 shipped 4/4 + post-merge hygiene. Pi runs systemd eclipse-obd in --simulate mode (still). HardwareManager + UpsMonitor + GPIO button live. python-OBD library resolves correctly post US-187 rename. Pygame renders to OSOYOO 3.5" via X11 (DISPLAY=:0 + XAUTHORITY required — Session 21 surprise; baked into US-170 + render scripts this sprint).
SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy at 10.27.27.10. No server changes this sprint.
SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-run (created by PM from main@1b740e3). Marcus committed this setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): Sprint 13 = 5 stories (2M + 3S). Tighter than Sprint 11 (7 stories) deliberately — Run phase has hardware unknowns (BT can be finicky, first real OBD reads may surface PID-support quirks specific to the Eclipse's ECU). US-169 (UPS in-car ignition cycles) and the B-043 follow-on US-189/US-190 are explicitly Sprint 14 candidates.

EXECUTION ORDER: US-167 BT pairing FIRST — gateway story, US-168 + US-170 wait on it. While US-167 is in flight, US-188 (WiFi detection, bench-only) and US-191 (flat-file replay harness, bench-only) can progress in parallel on Windows. Once US-167 lands → US-168 (live idle data) → US-170 (display).

FROM TONIGHT'S BENCH PROOF (Session 21, 2026-04-18): three pre-car checks all PASS. Sync e2e proven (523 rows pushed Pi → server, lastSync timestamp updated). HDMI render proven (6 gauges visible on OSOYOO via X11). UPS power-source detection proven (2 unplug/replug cycles, VCELL trend caught every transition in ~2-4s). The car move is unblocked.

BUNDLED HOUSEKEEPING (small fixes, NOT full stories — Ralph touches as he goes): (a) API_KEY env wire-up properly (Sprint 11 leak — server .env + Pi .env need matching key, deploy scripts pass-through). (b) OBD_BT_MAC config default — sync_now.py choked on missing default; needs sane fallback. (c) pygame DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY in render scripts AND in eclipse-obd.service [Service] block (US-170 covers the service-file part). (d) Multi-HDMI dev workflow note — xrandr forces output for bench dev (in-car single-display moot).
STOP EARLY IF SSH OR BT BREAKS. Any story depending on Pi SSH must verify connectivity first. US-167 BT pairing has its own STOP conditions — see story body.

stories.title: Bluetooth OBD-II pairing with OBDLink LX (gateway story)
stories.intent: First-time pair the OBDLink LX (MAC 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB) to the Pi. Establish a serial port binding (/dev/rfcomm0). python-OBD's obd.OBD() constructor connects via that serial port. Pairing survives reboot. Config-driven (MAC in config.json, never hardcoded — per B-044 standing rule). GATEWAY story — US-168, US-170, all Run-phase work waits on this.

filesToTouch: deploy/deploy-pi.sh (verify bluetooth/bluez packages already in apt list from Sprint 10 — they should be)
scripts/pair_obdlink.sh (new — CIO-runnable BT pairing driver)
src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py (if connection logic needs BT-specific tuning)
src/pi/obdii/config/loader.py or similar (read OBD_BT_MAC from config)
config.json (pi.bluetooth section already exists; verify defaults)
.env.example (OBD_BT_MAC entry + comment)
tests/pi/obdii/test_obd_connection_bt.py (mocked BT path test, no real hardware)
specs/architecture.md (BT pairing flow paragraph)
docs/testing.md (CIO BT-pairing walkthrough)

groundingRefs.unit: OBDLink LX Bluetooth MAC (CIO's specific dongle)

groundingRefs.source: Linux Bluetooth stack default for first paired device

groundingRefs.unit: default OBDLink LX BT pairing PIN (per OBDLink documentation; confirm with CIO)

acceptance: scripts/pair_obdlink.sh is a CIO-runnable bash driver that: (1) verifies bluetoothctl is available on the Pi, (2) scans for the OBDLink LX at the configured MAC, (3) initiates pairing (PIN-based if required), (4) sets up rfcomm binding to /dev/rfcomm0, (5) verifies binding survives a one-shot disconnect/reconnect, (6) writes BT MAC into the Pi's local .env OBD_BT_MAC if not already there
config.json pi.bluetooth.macAddress reads from ${OBD_BT_MAC} (already present per Sprint 11) — verify env var loads via secrets_loader
On the Pi: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'rfcomm show /dev/rfcomm0' returns the bound device with matching MAC

Pi smoke test: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 '~/obd2-venv/bin/python -c "import obd; c = obd.OBD(\"/dev/rfcomm0\", baudrate=38400); print(c.is_connected(), c.protocol_name())"' returns True + non-empty protocol name (likely ISO 9141-2 for the 1998 Eclipse)
Pairing survives Pi reboot: CIO reboots, waits ~60s, re-runs the smoke test, gets True. systemd-rfcomm or equivalent persists the binding (NOT manual rfcomm bind on every boot)

If BT pairing fails on first attempt, log captures bluetoothctl error stream verbatim — CIO can paste back to PM. Common failures: dongle not powered (engine off), wrong PIN, MAC typo, BT adapter disabled

Test: tests/pi/obdii/test_obd_connection_bt.py covers mocked BT path — connection-success, connection-timeout, BT-adapter-missing — without real hardware. Lets us regression-test on Windows + Pi without dongle present
Update specs/architecture.md Bluetooth section with the actual flow
Update docs/testing.md with a CIO 'how to re-pair after a battery yank' walkthrough
Completion note: bluetoothctl scan output, rfcomm bind output, smoke test output, reboot-survival evidence, any quirks (Eclipse ECU's protocol_name)
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'bluetoothctl info 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB' returns 'Paired: yes'
ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'rfcomm show' shows /dev/rfcomm0 bound to correct MAC
Smoke test python -c (obd.OBD connection) returns True with car ignition ON
Same smoke test returns False (gracefully — no exception) with ignition OFF
pytest tests/pi/obdii/ -v on Windows + Pi (mocked tests, hardware-free)
ruff check src/pi/obdii/ scripts/pair_obdlink.sh tests/pi/obdii/
invariants: Do NOT hardcode the OBDLink MAC — config-driven via OBD_BT_MAC env var (B-044 standing rule)
Do NOT call sudo from Python — sudo-required commands stay in the bash pairing script
Do NOT auto-pair without CIO confirmation — pairing is a security event
Do NOT modify the obd.OBD() call signature — python-OBD's API is stable; if connection fails, fix config not the call
If the Pi's Bluetooth adapter is missing or disabled, surface to CIO with clear error — don't silently proceed
Document the protocol the Eclipse uses (likely ISO 9141-2 K-Line per specs/obd2-research.md) for Spool
stopConditions: STOP if bluetoothctl scan doesn't see the OBDLink LX after 30s — possible causes: (a) dongle not in pairing mode (some need a button press), (b) car ignition off (dongle is bus-powered), (c) BT range issue, (d) MAC typo. Surface bluetoothctl scan output to PM rather than guessing
STOP if pairing succeeds but rfcomm bind fails — log dmesg output (Bluetooth kernel module errors)
STOP if obd.OBD() connects but is_connected() returns False after 30s — protocol negotiation issue. Capture full debug log. May need explicit protocol= parameter
STOP if reboot-survival fails (binding gone after reboot) — needs systemd unit to re-bind on boot. Surface to PM, may want a follow-up TD

stories.title: Live idle data verification — first real OBD reads
stories.intent: With the engine running at idle in the garage and US-167 paired, query the Eclipse's ECU. Capture 5+ readings of core PIDs, validate against grounded-knowledge ranges. Decode VIN. Data lands in Pi SQLite. This proves the FULL real-data path works for the first time.

filesToTouch: scripts/verify_live_idle.sh (new — CIO-runnable driver)
src/pi/obdii/data/realtime.py (only if real-vs-sim path needs unification)
tests/pi/obdii/test_live_idle_ranges.py (range-check assertions)
data/regression/pi-inputs/eclipse_idle.db (captured snapshot for B-045 future fixture)
docs/testing.md (CIO live-idle walkthrough)
specs/grounded-knowledge.md (refine ranges with actual Eclipse readings)

acceptance: scripts/verify_live_idle.sh: SSH gate → confirm BT bound → confirm engine running (CIO checklist) → instantiate obd.OBD() → query supported PIDs → query 5 readings each of RPM/Coolant/IAT/Battery V/STFT/Speed at ~1Hz → range-check vs grounded-knowledge → query VIN + NHTSA decode + match → write all readings to ~/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db realtime_data → print PASS/FAIL summary
Each parameter falls within grounded-knowledge expected range (or PARTIAL with logged variance — NOT silent acceptance)
VIN decode returns 1998 Mitsubishi Eclipse GST. If mismatch, flag as anomaly but don't fail story (could be MEMORY.md error OR scrambled VIN)
Speed reads 0 mph at idle (sanity)
Supported PIDs captured: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'cat ~/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/eclipse_supported_pids.txt' shows actual ECU's PID inventory (Spool wants this for ECMLink Phase 2)
Data written to local SQLite: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM realtime_data after the drill > 30 (5 readings × 6 PIDs)
Capture also generates data/regression/pi-inputs/eclipse_idle.db — a sanitized snapshot as a B-045 future fixture
tests/pi/obdii/test_live_idle_ranges.py defines range-check assertions in code (not just bash) for future re-use against fixtures
Update specs/grounded-knowledge.md with measured values that refine documented ranges (e.g., 'measured 2026-04-XX: 880 ± 10 RPM at warm idle')

verification: bash scripts/verify_live_idle.sh exits 0 with CIO at the car, engine warm + idling
pytest tests/pi/obdii/test_live_idle_ranges.py -v on Windows (range logic, no hardware)

Do NOT touch the simulator code path — goal is REAL data; if real fails, file the issue, don't fall back to sim quietly

stories.title: WiFi / home-network detection (B-043 building block)
stories.intent: Bench-only foundation work for B-043 auto-sync. Build a small module that answers 'are we on DeathStarWiFi?' and 'can we reach Chi-Srv-01?' Returns a HomeNetworkState enum. Logged on transitions. Used by the future PowerLossOrchestrator (US-189, Sprint 14). NO orchestration logic in this story — just detection.

filesToTouch: src/pi/network/ (new package)
src/pi/network/__init__.py
src/pi/network/home_detector.py (the module)
src/common/config/validator.py (defaults for new pi.homeNetwork section)
config.json (pi.homeNetwork.{ssid, subnet, pingTimeoutSeconds, serverPingPath})
tests/pi/network/test_home_detector.py (unit + mocked-subprocess)
specs/architecture.md (home-network detection paragraph)
filesToRead: src/pi/sync/client.py (where SyncClient does HTTP — same baseUrl pattern)

acceptance: src/pi/network/home_detector.py exposes: HomeNetworkState enum (AT_HOME_SERVER_REACHABLE, AT_HOME_SERVER_DOWN, AWAY, UNKNOWN), isAtHomeWifi() -> bool, isServerReachable() -> bool, getHomeNetworkState() -> HomeNetworkState
isAtHomeWifi() checks BOTH SSID match (via 'iwgetid -r') AND subnet match (via Pi's IP in 10.27.27.0/24). True only if both pass — defense in depth
isServerReachable() does GET {baseUrl}/api/v1/ping with X-API-Key header + bounded timeout (default 3s). Returns True on 2xx, False otherwise. Does NOT raise on network errors

getHomeNetworkState() composes the two: at home + server up → AT_HOME_SERVER_REACHABLE, at home + server down → AT_HOME_SERVER_DOWN, not at home → AWAY. UNKNOWN if SSID detection fails (no WiFi adapter)

Config defaults in pi.homeNetwork: ssid='DeathStarWiFi', subnet='10.27.27.0/24', pingTimeoutSeconds=3, serverPingPath='/api/v1/ping'. ConfigValidator enforces shape (string SSID, valid CIDR, positive timeout)
Tests cover: at-home-server-up, at-home-server-down, away (SSID mismatch), away (no WiFi adapter — iwgetid fails), unknown (subprocess timeout). 5+ tests minimum
Logging: state changes from one HomeNetworkState to another get logged at INFO level with both old and new state
Completion note: state-table sample for each scenario (mocked), test counts, ruff result. Does NOT need live-Pi verification — that's B-043 follow-on (US-189)
verification: pytest tests/pi/network/ -v on Windows + Pi
python validate_config.py succeeds with new pi.homeNetwork section
ruff check src/pi/network/ src/common/config/ tests/pi/network/
invariants: Do NOT call sudo from Python — iwgetid + ip commands run as user-level reads
Do NOT block on subprocess — bounded timeouts on every shell-out
Do NOT make this module import any Pi-only hardware libs — must be testable on Windows (mocking subprocess)
Do NOT use SyncClient for the ping check — circular dep. Use plain urllib or requests with bounded timeout
stopConditions: STOP if iwgetid is unavailable on Pi (BT-only / wired-only setups) — fall back to subnet-only detection, log warning, surface to PM if persists
STOP if test ergonomics get ugly because subprocess-mocking is hard — propose redesign (e.g., dependency-injected detector functions) before going down a rabbit hole
